sections.Description: The stronger variant of the regularHornetwith more health, damage and range. Flying mobility lets it move freely through mountains, cities and the players units. It cannot be drowned, so has to be killed through damage and damaging effects instead.
sections.Strategy:
The Alpha Hornet's ability to attack two tiles for two damage makes them a menace to the grid, possibly restricting the directions the player can safely displace them in. Combined with their very high movement of 5 tiles, they are very dangerous and should be killed at the earliest opportunity that is at all reasonable.
They have enough health to survive most un-upgraded attacks, and can take 2 actions in the early-game to eliminate entirely. Even in the late-game when the player can have access to attacks that do 4 base damage, their high mobility and flight lets them attack from water or pit tiles in tough angles, inaccessible corners, or restrict the players grounded Mechs from reaching other enemies.
On the initial deployment turn, deploying two of your Mechs in a line can sometimes bait Alpha Hornets to attack your mechs instead of cities, which gives more breathing room for the first turn. Although they can fly, they still can't occupy tiles your own units are occupying, so it can also be useful to deploy your own mechs / allied units inside corners, or next to the middle city of a line with 3 cities. Always have a plan for how to dislodge an Alpha Hornet from faraway cities or narrow passageways, tiles which would otherwise be very annoying for the Alpha Hornet to enter.
If looking to kill them, any kind of pre-emptive or additive damage to soften them up makes a huge difference - fire, bump damage from other attacks or your own Mechs, or enemy fire can score additional damage on them; look forAlpha FirefliesandAlpha Beetlesto redirect and attack the Alpha Hornet. TheFirefly Leadercan do 4 damage too, killing them in one shot. Otherwise, attacks that both damage and move at the same time are essential for dealing with them, as are weapons that can either pull, teleport, or cancel the Alpha Hornet's attack entirely (such as freeze or smoke). Freezing them over water will not drown them as it will also create an ice tile, but shoving a frozen Alpha Hornet into the water will still drown it. Freezing an Alpha Hornet over pit will make it fall into the pit. Beware in the final mission, as you cannot freeze a Hornet flying above lava.

from

Description
[
]
The stronger variant of the regular
Hornet
with more health, damage and range. Flying mobility lets it move freely through mountains, cities and the players units. It cannot be drowned, so has to be killed through damage and damaging effects instead.
Strategy
[
]
The Alpha Hornet's ability to attack two tiles for two damage makes them a menace to the grid, possibly restricting the directions the player can safely displace them in. Combined with their very high movement of 5 tiles, they are very dangerous and should be killed at the earliest opportunity that is at all reasonable.
They have enough health to survive most un-upgraded attacks, and can take 2 actions in the early-game to eliminate entirely. Even in the late-game when the player can have access to attacks that do 4 base damage, their high mobility and flight lets them attack from water or pit tiles in tough angles, inaccessible corners, or restrict the players grounded Mechs from reaching other enemies.
On the initial deployment turn, deploying two of your Mechs in a line can sometimes bait Alpha Hornets to attack your mechs instead of cities, which gives more breathing room for the first turn. Although they can fly, they still can't occupy tiles your own units are occupying, so it can also be useful to deploy your own mechs / allied units inside corners, or next to the middle city of a line with 3 cities. Always have a plan for how to dislodge an Alpha Hornet from faraway cities or narrow passageways, tiles which would otherwise be very annoying for the Alpha Hornet to enter.
If looking to kill them, any kind of pre-emptive or additive damage to soften them up makes a huge difference - fire, bump damage from other attacks or your own Mechs, or enemy fire can score additional damage on them; look for
Alpha Fireflies
and
Alpha Beetles
to redirect and attack the Alpha Hornet. The
Firefly Leader
can do 4 damage too, killing them in one shot. Otherwise, attacks that both damage and move at the same time are essential for dealing with them, as are weapons that can either pull, teleport, or cancel the Alpha Hornet's attack entirely (such as freeze or smoke). Freezing them over water will not drown them as it will also create an ice tile, but shoving a frozen Alpha Hornet into the water will still drown it. Freezing an Alpha Hornet over pit will make it fall into the pit. Beware in the final mission, as you cannot freeze a Hornet flying above lava.
